candidate_problem_record.subject: math
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, $D$ is a point on $AB$, $DF$ intersects $AC$ at point $E$, $DE=FE$, $FC \parallel AB$, $AB=5$, $BD=1$. What is the length of $CF$?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Given $FC\parallel AB$,\\
it follows that $\angle F = \angle ADE$, $\angle FCE = \angle A$,\\
In $\triangle CFE$ and $\triangle ADE$,\\
$\left\{ \begin{array}{l} \angle F = \angle ADE \\ \angle FCE = \angle A \\ FE = DE \end{array} \right.$,\\
thus $\triangle CFE \cong \triangle ADE$ (AAS),\\
thus $CF = AD$,\\
since $AB = 5$, $BD = 1$,\\
it follows that $AD = AB - BD = 5 - 1 = 4$,\\
thus $CF = \boxed{4}$,\\
hence the length of $CF$ is $4$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集被视为具备较稳定的多解潜力，可进入更强的多解挖掘链路。
